hypothesis: Concrete eBPF Bytecode Modification:** Attach an eBPF program to a tracepoint within the kernel's sort execution path. The eBPF bytecode maintains an Exponentially Weighted Moving Average (EWMA) of swap distances (representing volatility) in an eBPF map. Modify the bytecode execution flow using a tail call (`bpf_tail_call`) to dynamically swap the partitioning function pointer: if the EWMA falls below threshold $\theta$ (the crossover point) or the subarray size $N < 64$, the bytecode injects a JIT-compiled branchless block-sorting kernel (`BPF_ALU64_IMM` instructions optimized for conditional moves without branching) to eliminate branch misprediction costs.
